description: A walker can head toward the east without claiming to reach it. 'wea' marks that direction or orientation and leaves the outcome open. It works with physical landmarks and extends to times or abstract aims. 'kau' (ALL) instead makes the landmark the promised destination.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then unround into middle-height front 'e'. Hold the syllable break while lowering the jaw into open central 'a'. Stress 'we'.
usage_notes: The phrase begins with 'wea' and then names the landmark. The verb or later context can say whether the movement eventually reaches it.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A path toward awakening is still a direction rather than an achieved state; 'wea' keeps those claims distinct.
